author: @TerryGilsenan
text: Rob, You will find that running a current through a bearing is an imperfect contact and that at higher voltage and current is prone to arcing and erosion/deposition, that will destroy the bearing. Auto makers will never use a bearing as a current path.

author: @kreynolds1123
text:
Ways of getting electricity to a rotor 1) slip rings for AC or DC, 2) commutator for AC or DC, 3) inductive coupling for only AC 4) capacitive coupling for AC only. 

A coil inside a coil can act like a transformer. While capacitive coupling works better with high voltages and high freqency.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate

author: @kevinleebailey
text: You could add a handle to the motor shaft and attach the arm to the case of the motor. Then spin the motor and not the shaft. There would be no need for bearing slip ring. Just take two wires straight off the back of the motor to the LED. Or use the arm like you already have as one of the connections. Put one of your super capacitors on there and your away. The bearing slip ring could be used on wind turbines.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nice idea mate - effectively spin the magnet and not the coil

author: @kreynolds1123
text: One led lights for one half wave, and a second LED wired in reverse would light during the other half wave.

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text:
Thats fun. I'm not sure what interference were seeing. There some from the led but there another factor in the filming frames/second, refresh rate of monitors and some others.
Use a stepper motor ! The pattern you'll be seeing is directly related to the step angle from your motor. That'll give an amazing result.
